merged_events.event_description: Diplomatic relations established when U.S. Ambassador to Australia Marshall Green presented credentials to Nauru, remaining resident at Canberra.

evidence.quote: Diplomatic relations were established on October 24, 1976, when U.S. Ambassador to Australia Marshall Green presented his credentials to Nauru; he remained resident at Canberra.

merged_events.event_description: Accreditation of U.S. Ambassador to Nauru changed from Canberra to Suva; U.S. Ambassador to Fiji Don L. Gevirtz presented credentials, remaining resident at Suva.

evidence.quote: On April 24, 1996, U.S. Ambassador to Fiji Don L. Gevirtz presented his credentials; he remained resident at Suva.

merged_events.event_description: Establishment of nonresident ambassadorial relations with Nauru. Marshall Green, the first U.S. Ambassador to Nauru, presented credentials while based in Canberra.

evidence.quote: Marshall Green, Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1974-02-28. Credentials presented: 1974-10-26. Left Canberra 1975-07-31.

matched.notes: CSV date 1976-10-24 matches RDCR Event 0 exactly. POCOM Event 2 gives 1974-10-26 for Marshall Green's credential presentation, which conflicts with RDCR's explicit institutional date of 1976-10-24. Per source priority, RDCR wins since it provides an explicit establishment date. The CSV correctly follows RDCR.

missing_from_csv.event_description: Accreditation of U.S. Ambassador to Nauru changed from Canberra (Australia) to Suva (Fiji). U.S. Ambassador to Fiji Don L. Gevirtz presented credentials, remaining resident at Suva.
missing_from_csv.notes: The diplomatic status category remains 'Ambassador Nonresident' but the resident post changed from Canberra to Suva. This is a meaningful institutional change (different covering embassy) that may warrant a row in the CSV even though the status label is the same. RDCR explicitly documents this transition with a specific date.
